updatedBy: Local live fetch from public sources

sources.name: FRED
sources.label: Federal Reserve Economic Data
sources.note: Public CSV downloads from FRED graph endpoints; no API key required for the graph CSV route.

sources.name: Multpl
sources.label: Multpl valuation tables
sources.note: Public HTML tables for long-history S&P 500 valuation ratios such as Shiller CAPE, PE, earnings yield, and dividend yield.

sources.name: Siblis Research
sources.label: Siblis index valuation tables
sources.note: Public index valuation tables used for Russell 2000 PE, forward PE, and CAPE history.

sources.name: Yahoo Finance Chart
sources.label: Yahoo market chart endpoint
sources.note: Used only for optional live market series where FRED coverage is weak. The app degrades gracefully if blocked.

sources.name: U.S. Treasury FiscalData
sources.label: Treasury Fiscal Data API
sources.note: Open public REST API; reserved for extension points and source attribution.

sources.name: World Bank
sources.label: World Bank open data API
sources.note: Public country-level annual macro indicators including GDP growth, CPI inflation, unemployment, and current-account balances.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} general government gross debt to GDP
countryMetricTemplates.why: General government gross debt to GDP is a slow-moving fiscal fragility gauge; high or rising readings can reduce policy room and raise refinancing sensitivity.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} long-term government bond yield
countryMetricTemplates.why: Long-term government bond yields are the market price of sovereign refinancing pressure and duration risk.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} real GDP growth
countryMetricTemplates.why: Weak real GDP growth points to cyclical stress and a smaller income base for public and private debt.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} CPI inflation
countryMetricTemplates.why: High CPI inflation can force policy tightening, pressure real incomes, and destabilize local bonds and FX.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} unemployment rate
countryMetricTemplates.why: Rising unemployment is a direct read on domestic growth stress and political-economy pressure.

countryMetricTemplates.name: {country} current account balance to GDP
countryMetricTemplates.why: A weak current account can expose countries to funding, FX, and balance-of-payments pressure when global liquidity tightens.

scenarios.name: JPY carry unwind
scenarios.description: Yen funding trade pressure: yen strength, equity volatility, Nikkei stress, U.S. rate compression, and broad risk-off.

scenarios.name: Funding stress
scenarios.description: Bank funding and credit pressure across financial conditions, OAS, money-market rates, volatility, and Fed liquidity plumbing.

scenarios.name: Inflation shock
scenarios.description: Price pressure impulse from realized inflation, breakevens, oil, gasoline, natural gas, and inflation expectations.

scenarios.name: Growth scare
scenarios.description: Real-economy deterioration: labor stress, production weakness, retail softness, housing slowdown, and survey contraction.

scenarios.name: Liquidity drain
scenarios.description: Balance-sheet and reserve pressure from Fed QT, reserve balances, TGA, RRP, broad dollar strength, and high front-end rates.

scenarios.name: Cross-asset risk-off
scenarios.description: Market stress visible in volatility, credit spreads, equities, dollar strength, oil stress, and curve behavior.

scenarios.name: Valuation fragility
scenarios.description: Equity valuation pressure across CAPE, PE, earnings yield gaps, market-cap-to-GDP, and liquidity-normalized equity ratios.

scenarios.name: Sovereign refinancing
scenarios.description: Country-level debt fragility across government debt loads, long-term bond yields, and sovereign spread pressure.

scenarios.name: Country macro shock
scenarios.description: Large-country macro deterioration across growth, inflation, labor markets, and domestic policy stress.

scenarios.name: External vulnerability
scenarios.description: Balance-of-payments and FX pressure from current-account weakness, dollar strength, and country-specific external stress.

metrics.name: S&P 500 Shiller CAPE ratio
metrics.why: CAPE compares equity prices with a 10-year smoothed earnings base; very high readings flag expensive long-cycle equity valuation.

metrics.name: S&P 500 trailing PE ratio
metrics.why: Trailing PE captures how much investors pay for realized earnings; high readings can make markets more fragile to rate or earnings shocks.

metrics.name: S&P 500 earnings yield
metrics.why: Low earnings yield means investors receive little earnings compensation for equity risk.

metrics.name: S&P 500 dividend yield
metrics.why: Low dividend yield is a simple long-history valuation pressure gauge.

metrics.name: Russell 2000 index price
metrics.why: Russell 2000 price history anchors the small-cap valuation ratios from the same public table.

metrics.name: Russell 2000 trailing PE ratio
metrics.why: Russell 2000 trailing PE tracks how expensive U.S. small caps are relative to realized earnings.

metrics.name: Russell 2000 forward PE ratio
metrics.why: Russell 2000 forward PE shows small-cap valuation versus expected earnings, useful when trailing earnings are cyclically distorted.

metrics.name: Russell 2000 cyclically adjusted PE
metrics.why: Russell 2000 CAPE smooths earnings over the cycle and highlights small-cap valuation fragility.

metrics.name: Buffett indicator proxy
metrics.why: A public-data Buffett indicator proxy: nonfinancial corporate equity market value divided by nominal GDP.

metrics.name: Equity market value to M2
metrics.why: Normalizes equity market value by broad money; high readings can flag stretched equity valuation relative to liquidity.

metrics.name: Equity market value to Fed assets
metrics.why: Compares equity market value with the Fed balance sheet; rising readings can expose valuation dependence on liquidity conditions.

metrics.name: Fed model earnings yield gap
metrics.why: Compares S&P 500 earnings yield with the 10-year Treasury yield; low or negative gaps mean equities offer less yield cushion versus bonds.

metrics.name: Real earnings yield gap
metrics.why: Compares equity earnings yield with real Treasury yield; low readings flag valuation fragility when real rates are high.

metrics.name: Italy minus Germany 10-year government bond spread
metrics.why: Italy's spread to Germany is a direct market gauge of euro-area sovereign refinancing and fiscal-fragmentation risk.

metrics.name: France minus Germany 10-year government bond spread
metrics.why: France's spread to Germany helps catch fiscal credibility and euro-area core spread stress before it becomes a headline crisis.

metrics.name: United Kingdom minus Germany 10-year government bond spread
metrics.why: UK-gilt underperformance versus Germany can flag domestic fiscal, inflation, or pension-led duration stress.

metrics.name: Japan minus U.S. 10-year government bond spread
metrics.why: Japan-U.S. long-rate spread changes can interact with yen carry trades, hedging costs, and Japanese capital flows.

metrics.name: CBOE VIX
metrics.why: Equity implied volatility is the fastest public proxy for forced de-risking.

metrics.name: S&P 500
metrics.why: Large-cap U.S. equity price level; downside momentum matters more than high levels.

metrics.name: NASDAQ Composite
metrics.why: Tech-heavy equity beta; often amplifies rates and liquidity shocks.

metrics.name: Nikkei 225
metrics.why: Japanese equities are a useful lens on yen carry stress and local risk appetite.

metrics.name: 3-month Treasury yield
metrics.why: Front-end rates transmit Fed policy into funding and carry costs.

metrics.name: 2-year Treasury yield
metrics.why: Policy-sensitive rates repricing can hit equities, credit, and FX carry quickly.

metrics.name: 10-year Treasury yield
metrics.why: The long rate anchors global discount rates; fast moves in either direction can shock positioning.

metrics.name: 30-year Treasury yield
metrics.why: Long-duration yield extremes expose pension, mortgage convexity, and equity valuation pressure.

metrics.name: 10Y minus 2Y Treasury spread
metrics.why: Deep inversion or sudden steepening can flag recession risk or policy panic.

metrics.name: 10Y minus 3M Treasury spread
metrics.why: The 3m10y curve is a classic recession-risk signal when deeply inverted.

metrics.name: 10-year TIPS real yield
metrics.why: High real yields tighten financial conditions even when nominal yields look stable.

metrics.name: 10-year breakeven inflation
metrics.why: Market-implied inflation compensation can warn before realized inflation data updates.

metrics.name: 5-year breakeven inflation
metrics.why: The 5-year breakeven catches medium-term inflation scares faster than CPI prints.

metrics.name: Secured Overnight Financing Rate
metrics.why: SOFR is the core overnight secured funding rate; spikes matter for leverage.

metrics.name: Effective federal funds rate
metrics.why: Fed funds shows the actual overnight policy-rate anchor.

metrics.name: Interest on reserve balances
metrics.why: IORB is the administered rate floor for bank reserve opportunity cost.

metrics.name: CPI inflation
metrics.why: Headline CPI shocks can force policy repricing and risk-asset multiple compression.

metrics.name: Core CPI inflation
metrics.why: Core CPI is stickier and usually more important for Fed reaction risk.

metrics.name: PCE inflation
metrics.why: PCE is the Fed's preferred inflation gauge.

metrics.name: Core PCE inflation
metrics.why: Core PCE persistence is central to Fed reaction-function risk.

metrics.name: Producer price inflation
metrics.why: PPI can flag upstream price pressure before it fully reaches consumers.

metrics.name: University of Michigan inflation expectations
metrics.why: Household inflation expectations can influence wage and pricing behavior.

metrics.name: Unemployment rate
metrics.why: A rising unemployment rate is the slow but decisive recession confirmation signal.

metrics.name: Payroll employment 3m annualized growth
metrics.why: Payroll momentum turns before the level of employment looks alarming.

metrics.name: Initial jobless claims
metrics.why: Claims are one of the timeliest recession and layoff warnings.

metrics.name: Continuing jobless claims
metrics.why: Continuing claims show whether laid-off workers are finding jobs quickly.

metrics.name: Industrial production growth
metrics.why: Industrial production weakness catches cyclical slowdowns and inventory shocks.

metrics.name: Retail sales growth
metrics.why: Retail sales reveal consumer demand pressure before broad GDP revisions.

metrics.name: Housing starts
metrics.why: Housing starts are rate-sensitive and can flag policy drag early.

metrics.name: Building permits
metrics.why: Permits are a forward-looking read on residential construction.

metrics.name: University of Michigan consumer sentiment
metrics.why: Weak sentiment can expose demand fragility and political-economy stress.

metrics.name: OECD manufacturing confidence indicator
metrics.why: Manufacturing confidence is a timely survey proxy for cyclical demand and production stress.

metrics.name: 30-year mortgage rate
metrics.why: Mortgage rates transmit policy rates into housing affordability and construction.

metrics.name: U.S. high-yield OAS
metrics.why: High-yield spreads are one of the cleanest public credit-stress gauges.

metrics.name: U.S. corporate OAS
metrics.why: Broad corporate OAS captures the average compensation for credit risk.

metrics.name: BBB corporate OAS
metrics.why: BBB spreads sit near the investment-grade/high-yield migration boundary.

metrics.name: AAA corporate OAS
metrics.why: AAA spreads separate broad liquidity premium from lower-quality default risk.

metrics.name: Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index
metrics.why: NFCI aggregates leverage, risk, and credit conditions into one stress read.

metrics.name: Adjusted National Financial Conditions Index
metrics.why: Adjusted NFCI strips out contemporaneous macro conditions to isolate financial tightness.

metrics.name: St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index
metrics.why: This index is built to flag system-wide financial market stress.

metrics.name: Commercial and industrial loan growth
metrics.why: Weak C&I loan growth can show tighter credit creation.

metrics.name: Fed balance sheet growth
metrics.why: Balance-sheet contraction can remove liquidity cushion from duration and credit markets.

metrics.name: Reserve balances with Federal Reserve Banks
metrics.why: Low reserves can make funding markets more fragile.

metrics.name: Treasury General Account
metrics.why: A high TGA can drain bank reserves as Treasury cash sits at the Fed.

metrics.name: Overnight reverse repo
metrics.why: RRP extremes show how much money-market cash is parked at the Fed.

metrics.name: M2 money supply growth
metrics.why: Money supply extremes can map to inflation pressure or liquidity contraction.

metrics.name: Nominal broad U.S. dollar index
metrics.why: Dollar strength tightens global financial conditions and pressures dollar borrowers.

metrics.name: Japanese yen per U.S. dollar
metrics.why: A crowded weak-yen regime can unwind violently when USDJPY falls fast.

metrics.name: Chinese yuan per U.S. dollar
metrics.why: Yuan weakness can transmit China growth stress and dollar pressure globally.

metrics.name: U.S. dollar per euro
metrics.why: A falling EURUSD is often another expression of dollar funding pressure.

metrics.name: WTI crude oil
metrics.why: Oil spikes feed headline inflation and real-income shocks.

metrics.name: Brent crude oil
metrics.why: Brent is the global oil benchmark and captures non-U.S. supply shocks.

metrics.name: U.S. regular gasoline price
metrics.why: Gasoline is highly visible to households and can move inflation expectations.

metrics.name: Henry Hub natural gas
metrics.why: Natural gas spikes can hit utilities, industrial margins, and regional inflation.

metrics.name: Copper price
metrics.why: Copper is a cyclical demand and China-sensitive commodity signal.

metrics.name: CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index
metrics.why: Gold ETF implied volatility can flag safe-haven stress, inflation hedging demand, and commodity volatility shocks.
